prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for nitrobenzene (14 atoms) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', exact four-centre integrals, no density fitting) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Three SCF calculations were run: neutral (charge 0, mult 1), cation (charge +1, mult 2), and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges were computed via Mulliken (PySCF). The geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz file without re-optimisation at the DFT level.

All three SCF jobs converged. Integrity status: OK, trustworthy: true. Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000. No warnings were reported by the engine.

Headline result — most electrophilic site: Atom 4 (ipso carbon attached to –NO₂), f⁺ = 0.49641781.
Most nucleophilic site: Atom 5 (ortho carbon), f⁻ = 0.10728820.
The nitro oxygens (atoms 1 & 2) also carry high f⁺ (~0.473), consistent with the electron-withdrawing –NO₂ group. The dual descriptor is largest and positive at the ipso carbon (Δf = +0.51071), confirming its dominant electrophilic character.

CLI invocation: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout json --out nitrobenzene_fukui_dft.json

Output files: nitrobenzene_fukui_dft.json (result JSON); bar-chart plot at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260704-190321_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
